questions.question: What does semantic HTML mean?
choices: HTML where tags describe the meaning of the content they contain
HTML that is written in alphabetical order
HTML that includes CSS styling inline
questions.explanation: Semantic HTML uses tags that clearly describe the meaning and purpose of the content, such as <article>, <nav>, and <header>, rather than generic tags like <div>.

questions.question: What happens if you forget to close an HTML tag?
choices: The browser will always display an error message
The page may render incorrectly or unexpectedly
The file will not open at all
questions.explanation: Forgetting to close a tag can cause the browser to render content incorrectly, as it may apply the unclosed tag's effect to subsequent content.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an HTML validator?
choices: To make web pages load faster
To check HTML code for errors and standards compliance
To convert HTML to PDF format
questions.explanation: An HTML validator checks your code against the HTML standard to find errors like missing closing tags, improper nesting, or invalid attributes.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of proper nesting?
questions.explanation:
Proper nesting means tags are closed in the reverse order they were opened. The tag opens inside

, so it must close before

.

questions.question: The <!DOCTYPE ___> declaration tells the browser which version of HTML the page uses.
questions.explanation: <!DOCTYPE html> declares that the document is an HTML5 document. It should be the very first line in an HTML file.

questions.question: Using ___ (adding spaces or tabs) makes HTML code easier to read and maintain.
questions.explanation: Indentation visually shows the nesting structure of HTML elements, making the code much easier to read and debug.
